revision.comment: [[WP:AES|←]]新しいページ: 「{{神社 |名称 = 軽野神社 |画像={{画像募集中|cat=伊豆市}} |所在地 = [[静岡県]][[伊豆市]]松ケ瀬79 |地図 = |祭神 = [[事代主]] |社格 = [[式内社]]、郷社 |創建 = 不詳 |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = |神事 = }} '''軽野神社'''（かるのじんじゃ）は、[[静岡県]][[伊豆市]]の[[神社]]。 == 歴史 == 創建年代は不明である。ただ[[延喜式]]の…」
revision.text:
{{神社 |名称 = 軽野神社 |画像={{画像募集中|cat=伊豆市}} |所在地 = [[静岡県]][[伊豆市]]松ケ瀬79 |地図 = |祭神 = [[事代主]] |社格 = [[式内社]]、郷社 |創建 = 不詳 |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = |神事 = }} '''軽野神社'''（かるのじんじゃ）は、[[静岡県]][[伊豆市]]の[[神社]]。
== 歴史 == 創建年代は不明である。ただ[[延喜式]]の[[式内社]]に比定されていることから、少なくとも[[延喜]]年間（[[901年]] - [[923年]]）には既に存在していたものと推測される<ref name="散歩">静岡県日本史教育研究会 編『静岡県の歴史散歩（歴史散歩22）』山川出版社、2006年、67p</ref>。
『[[日本書紀]]』の[[応神天皇]]五年（[[274年]]）の条によれば、勅命により当社付近の木材で船を作らせたところ、船が'''軽'''く浮かび上がったことから、「'''軽野'''」という社名の由来になったという<ref name="散歩"></ref>。
== 交通アクセス == *[[大平インターチェンジ (静岡県)|大平IC]]より車3分。
== 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}}
== 参考文献 == *静岡県日本史教育研究会 編『静岡県の歴史散歩（歴史散歩22）』山川出版社、2006年
{{Pref-stub|pref=静岡県}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:かるのしんしや}} [[Category:静岡県の神社]] [[Category:伊豆市の歴史]] [[Category:伊豆市の建築物]] [[Category:静岡県の旧郷社]] [[Category:伊豆国の式内社]]

『[[日本書紀]]』の[[応神天皇]]五年（[[274年]]）の条によれば、勅命により当社付近の木材で船を作らせたところ、船が'''軽'''く浮かび上がったことから、「'''軽野'''」という社名の由来になったという<ref name="散歩"></ref>。

{{nihongo|'''Karuno Shrine'''|軽野神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Izu Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Karuno Shrine'''|軽野神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Izu Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Karuno Shrine'''|軽野神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Izu Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Karuno Shrine'''|軽野神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Izu Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Karuno Shrine'''|軽野神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Izu Province]].

revision.comment: 4 revisions imported
revision.text:
{{神社 |名称 = 軽野神社 |画像={{画像募集中|cat=伊豆市}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=11|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|34|56|35.4|N|138|55|54.6|E}}}} |所在地 = [[静岡県]][[伊豆市]]松ケ瀬79 |位置 = {{Coord|34|56|35.4|N|138|55|54.6|E|region:JP-22_scale:10000_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = [[事代主]] |社格 = [[式内社]]、旧[[郷社]] |創建 = 不詳 |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Shizuoka|label=軽野神社|coordinates= {{coord2|34|56|35.4|N|138|55|54.6|E}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} }} '''軽野神社'''（かるのじんじゃ）は、[[静岡県]][[伊豆市]]の[[神社]]。
== 歴史 == 創建年代は不明である。ただ[[延喜式]]の[[式内社]]に比定されていることから、少なくとも[[延喜]]年間（[[901年]] - [[923年]]）には既に存在していたものと推測される<ref name="散歩">静岡県日本史教育研究会 編『静岡県の歴史散歩（歴史散歩22）』山川出版社、2006年、67p</ref>。
『[[日本書紀]]』の[[応神天皇]]五年（[[274年]]）の条によれば、勅命により当社付近の木材で船を作らせたところ、船が'''軽'''く浮かび上がったことから、「'''軽野'''」という社名の由来になったという<ref name="散歩"></ref>。
== 交通アクセス == *[[大平インターチェンジ (静岡県)|大平IC]]より車3分。
== 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}}
== 参考文献 == *静岡県日本史教育研究会 編『静岡県の歴史散歩（歴史散歩22）』山川出版社、2006年
== 外部リンク == * [http://www.shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4402229 軽野神社] - 静岡県神社庁
{{Pref-stub|pref=静岡県}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:かるのしんしや}} [[Category:静岡県の神社]] [[Category:伊豆市の歴史]] [[Category:伊豆市の建築物]] [[Category:静岡県の旧郷社]] [[Category:伊豆国の式内社]]

[[JA:軽野神社 (伊豆市)]]
{{wikidata link|Q119929619}} [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]]
{{translated page|ja|軽野神社 (伊豆市)|version=103417611|comment=Imported full ja history}}[[Category:Automerged Japanese text]]
== Japanese Wikipedia content == {{神社 |名称 = 軽野神社 |画像={{画像募集中|cat=伊豆市}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=11|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|34|56|35.4|N|138|55|54.6|E}}}} |所在地 = [[静岡県]][[伊豆市]]松ケ瀬79 |位置 = {{Coord|34|56|35.4|N|138|55|54.6|E|region:JP-22_scale:10000_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = [[事代主]] |社格 = [[式内社]]、旧[[郷社]] |創建 = 不詳 |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Shizuoka|label=軽野神社|coordinates= {{coord2|34|56|35.4|N|138|55|54.6|E}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} }} '''軽野神社'''（かるのじんじゃ）は、[[静岡県]][[伊豆市]]の[[神社]]。
== 歴史 == 創建年代は不明である。ただ[[延喜式]]の[[式内社]]に比定されていることから、少なくとも[[延喜]]年間（[[901年]] - [[923年]]）には既に存在していたものと推測される<ref name="散歩">静岡県日本史教育研究会 編『静岡県の歴史散歩（歴史散歩22）』山川出版社、2006年、67p</ref>。
『[[日本書紀]]』の[[応神天皇]]五年（[[274年]]）の条によれば、勅命により当社付近の木材で船を作らせたところ、船が'''軽'''く浮かび上がったことから、「'''軽野'''」という社名の由来になったという<ref name="散歩"></ref>。
== 交通アクセス == *[[大平インターチェンジ (静岡県)|大平IC]]より車3分。
== 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}}
== 参考文献 == *静岡県日本史教育研究会 編『静岡県の歴史散歩（歴史散歩22）』山川出版社、2006年
== 外部リンク == * [http://www.shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4402229 軽野神社] - 静岡県神社庁

==End Japanese==

{{translated page|ja|軽野神社 (伊豆市)|version=103417611|comment=Imported full ja history}}[[Category:Automerged Japanese text]]
== Japanese Wikipedia content == {{神社 |名称 = 軽野神社 |画像={{画像募集中|cat=伊豆市}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=11|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|34|56|35.4|N|138|55|54.6|E}}}} |所在地 = [[静岡県]][[伊豆市]]松ケ瀬79 |位置 = {{Coord|34|56|35.4|N|138|55|54.6|E|region:JP-22_scale:10000_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = [[事代主]] |社格 = [[式内社]]、旧[[郷社]] |創建 = 不詳 |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Shizuoka|label=軽野神社|coordinates= {{coord2|34|56|35.4|N|138|55|54.6|E}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} }} '''軽野神社'''（かるのじんじゃ）は、[[静岡県]][[伊豆市]]の[[神社]]。
== 歴史 == 創建年代は不明である。ただ[[延喜式]]の[[式内社]]に比定されていることから、少なくとも[[延喜]]年間（[[901年]] - [[923年]]）には既に存在していたものと推測される<ref name="散歩">静岡県日本史教育研究会 編『静岡県の歴史散歩（歴史散歩22）』山川出版社、2006年、67p</ref>。
『[[日本書紀]]』の[[応神天皇]]五年（[[274年]]）の条によれば、勅命により当社付近の木材で船を作らせたところ、船が'''軽'''く浮かび上がったことから、「'''軽野'''」という社名の由来になったという<ref name="散歩"></ref>。
== 交通アクセス == *[[大平インターチェンジ (静岡県)|大平IC]]より車3分。
== 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}}
== 参考文献 == *静岡県日本史教育研究会 編『静岡県の歴史散歩（歴史散歩22）』山川出版社、2006年
== 外部リンク == * [http://www.shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4402229 軽野神社] - 静岡県神社庁
{{Pref-stub|pref=静岡県}} {{shinto-stub}}

'''軽野神社'''（かるのじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|静岡県|Q131320}}{{jalink|伊豆市|Q696840}}の{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}。

== 歴史 ==
創建年代は不明である。ただ{{jalink|延喜式|Q1342448}}の{{jalink|式内社}}に比定されていることから、少なくとも{{jalink|延喜|Q1374325}}年間（{{jalink|901年|Q23580}} - {{jalink|923年|Q30108}}）には既に存在していたものと推測される<ref name="散歩">静岡県日本史教育研究会 編『静岡県の歴史散歩（歴史散歩22）』山川出版社、2006年、67p</ref>。

『{{jalink|日本書紀|Q813935}}』の{{jalink|応神天皇|Q317997}}五年（{{jalink|274年|Q33512}}）の条によれば、勅命により当社付近の木材で船を作らせたところ、船が'''軽'''く浮かび上がったことから、「'''軽野'''」という社名の由来になったという<ref name="散歩"></ref>。

{{nihongo|'''Karuno Shrine'''|軽野神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Izu Province]].

== Japanese content ==
{{神社
|名称   = 軽野神社

|所在地 = {{jalink|静岡県|Q131320}}{{jalink|伊豆市|Q696840}}松ケ瀬79

'''軽野神社'''（かるのじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|静岡県|Q131320}}{{jalink|伊豆市|Q696840}}の{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}。

=== 歴史 ===
創建年代は不明である。ただ{{jalink|延喜式|Q1342448}}の{{jalink|式内社}}に比定されていることから、少なくとも{{jalink|延喜|Q1374325}}年間（{{jalink|901年|Q23580}} - {{jalink|923年|Q30108}}）には既に存在していたものと推測される<ref name="散歩">静岡県日本史教育研究会 編『静岡県の歴史散歩（歴史散歩22）』山川出版社、2006年、67p</ref>。

『{{jalink|日本書紀|Q813935}}』の{{jalink|応神天皇|Q317997}}五年（{{jalink|274年|Q33512}}）の条によれば、勅命により当社付近の木材で船を作らせたところ、船が'''軽'''く浮かび上がったことから、「'''軽野'''」という社名の由来になったという<ref name="散歩"></ref>。

=== 交通アクセス ===
*{{jalink|大平インターチェンジ (静岡県)|Q11435260|大平IC}}より車3分。

{{nihongo|'''Karuno Shrine'''|軽野神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Izu Province]].

== Japanese content ==
{{神社
|名称   = 軽野神社
|画像={{画像募集中|cat=伊豆市}}
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=11|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|34|56|35.4|N|138|55|54.6|E}}}}
|所在地 = {{jalink|静岡県|Q131320}}{{jalink|伊豆市|Q696840}}松ケ瀬79

{{coord2|34|56|35.4|N|138|55|54.6|E}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}}
}}
'''軽野神社'''（かるのじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|静岡県|Q131320}}{{jalink|伊豆市|Q696840}}の{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}。

=== 歴史 ===
創建年代は不明である。ただ{{jalink|延喜式|Q1342448}}の{{jalink|式内社}}に比定されていることから、少なくとも{{jalink|延喜|Q1374325}}年間（{{jalink|901年|Q23580}} - {{jalink|923年|Q30108}}）には既に存在していたものと推測される<ref name="散歩">静岡県日本史教育研究会 編『静岡県の歴史散歩（歴史散歩22）』山川出版社、2006年、67p</ref>。

『{{jalink|日本書紀|Q813935}}』の{{jalink|応神天皇|Q317997}}五年（{{jalink|274年|Q33512}}）の条によれば、勅命により当社付近の木材で船を作らせたところ、船が'''軽'''く浮かび上がったことから、「'''軽野'''」という社名の由来になったという<ref name="散歩"></ref>。

=== 交通アクセス ===
*{{jalink|大平インターチェンジ (静岡県)|Q11435260|大平IC}}より車3分。

{{nihongo|'''Karuno Shrine'''|軽野神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Izu Province]].

== Japanese content ==
{{神社
|名称   = 軽野神社
|画像={{画像募集中|cat=伊豆市}}
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=11|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|34|56|35.4|N|138|55|54.6|E}}}}
|所在地 = {{jalink|静岡県|Q131320}}{{jalink|伊豆市|Q696840}}松ケ瀬79

}}
'''軽野神社'''（かるのじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|静岡県|Q131320}}{{jalink|伊豆市|Q696840}}の{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}。

=== 歴史 ===
創建年代は不明である。ただ{{jalink|延喜式|Q1342448}}の{{jalink|式内社}}に比定されていることから、少なくとも{{jalink|延喜|Q1374325}}年間（{{jalink|901年|Q23580}} - {{jalink|923年|Q30108}}）には既に存在していたものと推測される<ref name="散歩">静岡県日本史教育研究会 編『静岡県の歴史散歩（歴史散歩22）』山川出版社、2006年、67p</ref>。

『{{jalink|日本書紀|Q813935}}』の{{jalink|応神天皇|Q317997}}五年（{{jalink|274年|Q33512}}）の条によれば、勅命により当社付近の木材で船を作らせたところ、船が'''軽'''く浮かび上がったことから、「'''軽野'''」という社名の由来になったという<ref name="散歩"></ref>。

=== 交通アクセス ===
*{{jalink|大平インターチェンジ (静岡県)|Q11435260|大平IC}}より車3分。

{{nihongo|'''Karuno Shrine'''|軽野神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Izu Province]].

'''軽野神社'''（かるのじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|静岡県|Q131320}}{{jalink|伊豆市|Q696840}}の{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}。

=== 歴史 ===
創建年代は不明である。ただ{{jalink|延喜式|Q1342448}}の{{jalink|式内社}}に比定されていることから、少なくとも{{jalink|延喜|Q1374325}}年間（{{jalink|901年|Q23580}} - {{jalink|923年|Q30108}}）には既に存在していたものと推測される<ref name="散歩">静岡県日本史教育研究会 編『静岡県の歴史散歩（歴史散歩22）』山川出版社、2006年、67p</ref>。

『{{jalink|日本書紀|Q813935}}』の{{jalink|応神天皇|Q317997}}五年（{{jalink|274年|Q33512}}）の条によれば、勅命により当社付近の木材で船を作らせたところ、船が'''軽'''く浮かび上がったことから、「'''軽野'''」という社名の由来になったという<ref name="散歩"></ref>。

=== 交通アクセス ===
*{{jalink|大平インターチェンジ (静岡県)|Q11435260|大平IC}}より車3分。

{{nihongo|'''Karuno Shrine'''|軽野神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Izu Province]].

'''軽野神社'''（かるのじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|静岡県|Q131320}}{{jalink|伊豆市|Q696840}}の{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}。

=== 歴史 ===
創建年代は不明である。ただ{{jalink|延喜式|Q1342448}}の{{jalink|式内社}}に比定されていることから、少なくとも{{jalink|延喜|Q1374325}}年間（{{jalink|901年|Q23580}} - {{jalink|923年|Q30108}}）には既に存在していたものと推測される<ref name="散歩">静岡県日本史教育研究会 編『静岡県の歴史散歩（歴史散歩22）』山川出版社、2006年、67p</ref>。

『{{jalink|日本書紀|Q813935}}』の{{jalink|応神天皇|Q317997}}五年（{{jalink|274年|Q33512}}）の条によれば、勅命により当社付近の木材で船を作らせたところ、船が'''軽'''く浮かび上がったことから、「'''軽野'''」という社名の由来になったという<ref name="散歩"></ref>。

=== 交通アクセス ===
*{{jalink|大平インターチェンジ (静岡県)|Q11435260|大平IC}}より車3分。

== Japanese content ==

'''軽野神社'''（かるのじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|静岡県|Q131320}}{{jalink|伊豆市|Q696840}}の{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}。

=== 歴史 ===
創建年代は不明である。ただ{{jalink|延喜式|Q1342448}}の{{jalink|式内社}}に比定されていることから、少なくとも{{jalink|延喜|Q1374325}}年間（{{jalink|901年|Q23580}} - {{jalink|923年|Q30108}}）には既に存在していたものと推測される<ref name="散歩">静岡県日本史教育研究会 編『静岡県の歴史散歩（歴史散歩22）』山川出版社、2006年、67p</ref>。

『{{jalink|日本書紀|Q813935}}』の{{jalink|応神天皇|Q317997}}五年（{{jalink|274年|Q33512}}）の条によれば、勅命により当社付近の木材で船を作らせたところ、船が'''軽'''く浮かび上がったことから、「'''軽野'''」という社名の由来になったという<ref name="散歩"></ref>。

=== 交通アクセス ===
*{{jalink|大平インターチェンジ (静岡県)|Q11435260|大平IC}}より車3分。

{{nihongo|'''Karuno Shrine'''|軽野神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Izu Province]].

== Japanese content ==
{{神社
|名称   = 軽野神社
|画像={{画像募集中|cat=伊豆市}}
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=11|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|34|56|35.4|N|138|55|54.6|E}}}}
|所在地 = {{jalink|静岡県|Q131320}}{{jalink|伊豆市|Q696840}}松ケ瀬79

{{coord2|34|56|35.4|N|138|55|54.6|E}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}}
}}
'''軽野神社'''（かるのじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|静岡県|Q131320}}{{jalink|伊豆市|Q696840}}の{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}。

=== 歴史 ===
創建年代は不明である。ただ{{jalink|延喜式|Q1342448}}の{{jalink|式内社}}に比定されていることから、少なくとも{{jalink|延喜|Q1374325}}年間（{{jalink|901年|Q23580}} - {{jalink|923年|Q30108}}）には既に存在していたものと推測される<ref name="散歩">静岡県日本史教育研究会 編『静岡県の歴史散歩（歴史散歩22）』山川出版社、2006年、67p</ref>。

『{{jalink|日本書紀|Q813935}}』の{{jalink|応神天皇|Q317997}}五年（{{jalink|274年|Q33512}}）の条によれば、勅命により当社付近の木材で船を作らせたところ、船が'''軽'''く浮かび上がったことから、「'''軽野'''」という社名の由来になったという<ref name="散歩"></ref>。

=== 交通アクセス ===
*{{jalink|大平インターチェンジ (静岡県)|Q11435260|大平IC}}より車3分。

{{nihongo|'''Karuno Shrine'''|軽野神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Izu Province]].

'''Karuno Shrine''' (かるのじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} in {{jalink|Izu, Shizuoka|Q696840}}, {{jalink|Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320}}.

=== History ===
Its year of foundation is unknown. However, since it is identified as a {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} in the {{jalink|Engishiki|Q1342448}}, it is presumed to have already existed at least by the {{jalink|Engi|Q1374325}} era ({{jalink|901|Q23580}}-{{jalink|923|Q30108}})<ref name="散歩">Shizuoka Prefecture Japanese History Education Research Society (ed.), ''A Historical Walk of Shizuoka Prefecture (Historical Walk 22)'', Yamakawa Publishing, 2006, p.67</ref>.

According to the entry for year 5 of {{jalink|Emperor Ōjin|Q317997}} ({{jalink|274|Q33512}}) in the ''{{jalink|Nihon Shoki|Q813935}}'', timber from near this shrine was used by imperial command to build a boat, and because the boat floated up "lightly," the shrine name "Karuno" ("light field") is said to derive from that<ref name="散歩"></ref>.

=== Access ===
* About 3 minutes by car from {{jalink|Ōdaira Interchange (Shizuoka)|Q11435260|Ōdaira IC}}.

=== Notes ===

'''Karuno Shrine''' (かるのじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} in {{jalink|Izu, Shizuoka|Q696840}}, {{jalink|Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320}}.

=== History ===
Its year of foundation is unknown. However, since it is identified as a {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} in the {{jalink|Engishiki|Q1342448}}, it is presumed to have already existed at least by the {{jalink|Engi|Q1374325}} era ({{jalink|901|Q23580}}-{{jalink|923|Q30108}})<ref name="散歩">Shizuoka Prefecture Japanese History Education Research Society (ed.), ''A Historical Walk of Shizuoka Prefecture (Historical Walk 22)'', Yamakawa Publishing, 2006, p.67</ref>.

According to the entry for year 5 of {{jalink|Emperor Ōjin|Q317997}} ({{jalink|274|Q33512}}) in the ''{{jalink|Nihon Shoki|Q813935}}'', timber from near this shrine was used by imperial command to build a boat, and because the boat floated up "lightly," the shrine name "Karuno" ("light field") is said to derive from that<ref name="散歩"></ref>.

=== Access ===
* About 3 minutes by car from {{jalink|Ōdaira Interchange (Shizuoka)|Q11435260|Ōdaira IC}}.

=== Notes ===

== Japanese content ==
{{神社
|名称   = Karuno Shrine
|画像={{画像募集中|cat=伊豆市}}
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=11|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|34|56|35.4|N|138|55|54.6|E}}}}
|所在地 = {{jalink|Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320}}{{jalink|Izu, Shizuoka|Q696840}} Matsugase 79
|位置 = {{Coord|34|56|35.4|N|138|55|54.6|E|region:JP-22_scale:10000_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}
|祭神   = {{jalink|Kotoshironushi|Q8009611}}

{{coord2|34|56|35.4|N|138|55|54.6|E}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}}
}}
'''Karuno Shrine''' (かるのじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} in {{jalink|Izu, Shizuoka|Q696840}}, {{jalink|Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320}}.

=== History ===
Its year of foundation is unknown. However, since it is identified as a {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} in the {{jalink|Engishiki|Q1342448}}, it is presumed to have already existed at least by the {{jalink|Engi|Q1374325}} era ({{jalink|901|Q23580}}-{{jalink|923|Q30108}})<ref name="散歩">Shizuoka Prefecture Japanese History Education Research Society (ed.), ''A Historical Walk of Shizuoka Prefecture (Historical Walk 22)'', Yamakawa Publishing, 2006, p.67</ref>.

According to the entry for year 5 of {{jalink|Emperor Ōjin|Q317997}} ({{jalink|274|Q33512}}) in the ''{{jalink|Nihon Shoki|Q813935}}'', timber from near this shrine was used by imperial command to build a boat, and because the boat floated up "lightly," the shrine name "Karuno" ("light field") is said to derive from that<ref name="散歩"></ref>.

=== Access ===
* About 3 minutes by car from {{jalink|Ōdaira Interchange (Shizuoka)|Q11435260|Ōdaira IC}}.

=== Notes ===
{{脚注ヘルプ}}
{{Reflist}}

=== References ===

'''Karuno Shrine''' (かるのじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} in {{jalink|Izu, Shizuoka|Q696840}}, {{jalink|Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320}}.

=== History ===
Its year of foundation is unknown. However, since it is identified as a {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} in the {{jalink|Engishiki|Q1342448}}, it is presumed to have already existed at least by the {{jalink|Engi|Q1374325}} era ({{jalink|901|Q23580}}-{{jalink|923|Q30108}})<ref name="散歩">Shizuoka Prefecture Japanese History Education Research Society (ed.), ''A Historical Walk of Shizuoka Prefecture (Historical Walk 22)'', Yamakawa Publishing, 2006, p.67</ref>.

According to the entry for year 5 of {{jalink|Emperor Ōjin|Q317997}} ({{jalink|274|Q33512}}) in the ''{{jalink|Nihon Shoki|Q813935}}'', timber from near this shrine was used by imperial command to build a boat, and because the boat floated up "lightly," the shrine name "Karuno" ("light field") is said to derive from that<ref name="散歩"></ref>.

=== Access ===
* About 3 minutes by car from {{jalink|Ōdaira Interchange (Shizuoka)|Q11435260|Ōdaira IC}}.

=== Notes ===
{{脚注ヘルプ}}

'''Karuno Shrine''' (かるのじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} in {{jalink|Izu, Shizuoka|Q696840}}, {{jalink|Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320}}.

=== History ===
Its year of foundation is unknown. However, since it is identified as a {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} in the {{jalink|Engishiki|Q1342448}}, it is presumed to have already existed at least by the {{jalink|Engi|Q1374325}} era ({{jalink|901|Q23580}}-{{jalink|923|Q30108}})<ref name="散歩">Shizuoka Prefecture Japanese History Education Research Society (ed.), ''A Historical Walk of Shizuoka Prefecture (Historical Walk 22)'', Yamakawa Publishing, 2006, p.67</ref>.

According to the entry for year 5 of {{jalink|Emperor Ōjin|Q317997}} ({{jalink|274|Q33512}}) in the ''{{jalink|Nihon Shoki|Q813935}}'', timber from near this shrine was used by imperial command to build a boat, and because the boat floated up "lightly," the shrine name "Karuno" ("light field") is said to derive from that<ref name="散歩"></ref>.

=== Access ===
* About 3 minutes by car from {{jalink|Ōdaira Interchange (Shizuoka)|Q11435260|Ōdaira IC}}.

=== Notes ===

'''Karuno Shrine''' (かるのじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} in {{jalink|Izu, Shizuoka|Q696840}}, {{jalink|Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320}}.

=== History ===
Its year of foundation is unknown. However, since it is identified as a {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} in the {{jalink|Engishiki|Q1342448}}, it is presumed to have already existed at least by the {{jalink|Engi|Q1374325}} era ({{jalink|901|Q23580}}-{{jalink|923|Q30108}})<ref name="散歩">Shizuoka Prefecture Japanese History Education Research Society (ed.), ''A Historical Walk of Shizuoka Prefecture (Historical Walk 22)'', Yamakawa Publishing, 2006, p.67</ref>.

According to the entry for year 5 of {{jalink|Emperor Ōjin|Q317997}} ({{jalink|274|Q33512}}) in the ''{{jalink|Nihon Shoki|Q813935}}'', timber from near this shrine was used by imperial command to build a boat, and because the boat floated up "lightly," the shrine name "Karuno" ("light field") is said to derive from that<ref name="散歩"></ref>.

=== Access ===
* About 3 minutes by car from {{jalink|Ōdaira Interchange (Shizuoka)|Q11435260|Ōdaira IC}}.

=== Notes ===

{{nihongo|'''Karuno Shrine'''|軽野神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Izu Province]].

'''Karuno Shrine''' (かるのじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} in {{jalink|Izu, Shizuoka|Q696840}}, {{jalink|Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320}}.

=== History ===
Its year of foundation is unknown. However, since it is identified as a {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} in the {{jalink|Engishiki|Q1342448}}, it is presumed to have already existed at least by the {{jalink|Engi|Q1374325}} era ({{jalink|901|Q23580}}-{{jalink|923|Q30108}})<ref name="散歩">Shizuoka Prefecture Japanese History Education Research Society (ed.), ''A Historical Walk of Shizuoka Prefecture (Historical Walk 22)'', Yamakawa Publishing, 2006, p.67</ref>.

According to the entry for year 5 of {{jalink|Emperor Ōjin|Q317997}} ({{jalink|274|Q33512}}) in the ''{{jalink|Nihon Shoki|Q813935}}'', timber from near this shrine was used by imperial command to build a boat, and because the boat floated up "lightly," the shrine name "Karuno" ("light field") is said to derive from that<ref name="散歩"></ref>.

=== Access ===
* About 3 minutes by car from {{jalink|Ōdaira Interchange (Shizuoka)|Q11435260|Ōdaira IC}}.

=== Notes ===

{{nihongo|'''Karuno Shrine'''|軽野神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Izu Province]].

|名称   = Karuno Shrine

'''Karuno Shrine''' (かるのじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} in {{jalink|Izu, Shizuoka|Q696840}}, {{jalink|Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320}}.

=== History ===
Its year of foundation is unknown. However, since it is identified as a {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} in the {{jalink|Engishiki|Q1342448}}, it is presumed to have already existed at least by the {{jalink|Engi|Q1374325}} era ({{jalink|901|Q23580}}-{{jalink|923|Q30108}})<ref name="散歩">Shizuoka Prefecture Japanese History Education Research Society (ed.), ''A Historical Walk of Shizuoka Prefecture (Historical Walk 22)'', Yamakawa Publishing, 2006, p.67</ref>.

According to the entry for year 5 of {{jalink|Emperor Ōjin|Q317997}} ({{jalink|274|Q33512}}) in the ''{{jalink|Nihon Shoki|Q813935}}'', timber from near this shrine was used by imperial command to build a boat, and because the boat floated up "lightly," the shrine name "Karuno" ("light field") is said to derive from that<ref name="散歩"></ref>.

=== Access ===
* About 3 minutes by car from {{jalink|Ōdaira Interchange (Shizuoka)|Q11435260|Ōdaira IC}}.

=== Notes ===
{{脚注ヘルプ}}
{{Reflist}}

=== References ===

== Japanese content ==
{{神社
|名称   = Karuno Shrine
|画像={{画像募集中|cat=伊豆市}}
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=11|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|34|56|35.4|N|138|55|54.6|E}}}}
|所在地 = {{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320|WD=Q131320}}{{ill|lt=Izu, Shizuoka|Q696840|WD=Q696840}} Matsugase 79
|位置 = {{Coord|34|56|35.4|N|138|55|54.6|E|region:JP-22_scale:10000_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}
|祭神   = {{ill|lt=Kotoshironushi|Q8009611|WD=Q8009611}}

{{coord2|34|56|35.4|N|138|55|54.6|E}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}}
}}
'''Karuno Shrine''' (かるのじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in {{ill|lt=Izu, Shizuoka|Q696840|WD=Q696840}}, {{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320|WD=Q131320}}.

=== History ===
Its year of foundation is unknown. However, since it is identified as a {{ill||lt=Shikinaisha|UNKNOWN}} in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448}}, it is presumed to have already existed at least by the {{ill|lt=Engi|Q1374325|WD=Q1374325}} era ({{ill|lt=901|Q23580|WD=Q23580}}-{{ill|lt=923|Q30108|WD=Q30108}})<ref name="散歩">Shizuoka Prefecture Japanese History Education Research Society (ed.), ''A Historical Walk of Shizuoka Prefecture (Historical Walk 22)'', Yamakawa Publishing, 2006, p.67</ref>.

According to the entry for year 5 of {{ill|lt=Emperor Ōjin|Q317997|WD=Q317997}} ({{ill|lt=274|Q33512|WD=Q33512}}) in the ''{{ill|lt=Nihon Shoki|Q813935|WD=Q813935}}'', timber from near this shrine was used by imperial command to build a boat, and because the boat floated up "lightly," the shrine name "Karuno" ("light field") is said to derive from that<ref name="散歩"></ref>.

=== Access ===
* About 3 minutes by car from {{ill|lt=Ōdaira Interchange (Shizuoka)|Q11435260|WD=Q11435260|lt=Ōdaira IC}}.

=== Notes ===

{{nihongo|'''Karuno Shrine'''|軽野神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Izu Province]].

'''Karuno Shrine''' (かるのじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in {{ill|lt=Izu, Shizuoka|Q696840|WD=Q696840}}, {{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320|WD=Q131320}}.

=== History ===
Its year of foundation is unknown. However, since it is identified as a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|UNKNOWN}} in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448}}, it is presumed to have already existed at least by the {{ill|lt=Engi|Q1374325|WD=Q1374325}} era ({{ill|lt=901|Q23580|WD=Q23580}}-{{ill|lt=923|Q30108|WD=Q30108}})<ref name="散歩">Shizuoka Prefecture Japanese History Education Research Society (ed.), ''A Historical Walk of Shizuoka Prefecture (Historical Walk 22)'', Yamakawa Publishing, 2006, p.67</ref>.

According to the entry for year 5 of {{ill|lt=Emperor Ōjin|Q317997|WD=Q317997}} ({{ill|lt=274|Q33512|WD=Q33512}}) in the ''{{ill|lt=Nihon Shoki|Q813935|WD=Q813935}}'', timber from near this shrine was used by imperial command to build a boat, and because the boat floated up "lightly," the shrine name "Karuno" ("light field") is said to derive from that<ref name="散歩"></ref>.

=== Access ===
* About 3 minutes by car from {{ill|lt=Ōdaira Interchange (Shizuoka)|Q11435260|WD=Q11435260|lt=Ōdaira IC}}.

=== History ===
Its year of foundation is unknown. However, since it is identified as a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN}} in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448}}, it is presumed to have already existed at least by the {{ill|lt=Engi|Q1374325|WD=Q1374325}} era ({{ill|lt=901|Q23580|WD=Q23580}}-{{ill|lt=923|Q30108|WD=Q30108}})<ref name="散歩">Shizuoka Prefecture Japanese History Education Research Society (ed.), ''A Historical Walk of Shizuoka Prefecture (Historical Walk 22)'', Yamakawa Publishing, 2006, p.67</ref>.

According to the entry for year 5 of {{ill|lt=Emperor Ōjin|Q317997|WD=Q317997}} ({{ill|lt=274|Q33512|WD=Q33512}}) in the ''{{ill|lt=Nihon Shoki|Q813935|WD=Q813935}}'', timber from near this shrine was used by imperial command to build a boat, and because the boat floated up "lightly," the shrine name "Karuno" ("light field") is said to derive from that<ref name="散歩"></ref>.

{{nihongo|'''Karuno Shrine'''|軽野神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Izu Province]].

'''Karuno Shrine''' (かるのじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in {{ill|lt=Izu, Shizuoka|Q696840|WD=Q696840}}, {{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320|WD=Q131320}}.

=== History ===
Its year of foundation is unknown. However, since it is identified as a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}} in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448}}, it is presumed to have already existed at least by the {{ill|lt=Engi|Q1374325|WD=Q1374325}} era ({{ill|lt=901|Q23580|WD=Q23580}}-{{ill|lt=923|Q30108|WD=Q30108}})<ref name="散歩">Shizuoka Prefecture Japanese History Education Research Society (ed.), ''A Historical Walk of Shizuoka Prefecture (Historical Walk 22)'', Yamakawa Publishing, 2006, p.67</ref>.

According to the entry for year 5 of {{ill|lt=Emperor Ōjin|Q317997|WD=Q317997}} ({{ill|lt=274|Q33512|WD=Q33512}}) in the ''{{ill|lt=Nihon Shoki|Q813935|WD=Q813935}}'', timber from near this shrine was used by imperial command to build a boat, and because the boat floated up "lightly," the shrine name "Karuno" ("light field") is said to derive from that<ref name="散歩"></ref>.

=== Access ===
* About 3 minutes by car from {{ill|lt=Ōdaira Interchange (Shizuoka)|Q11435260|WD=Q11435260|lt=Ōdaira IC}}.

'''Karuno Shrine''' (かるのじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in {{ill|lt=Izu, Shizuoka|Q696840|WD=Q696840}}, {{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320|WD=Q131320}}.

=== History ===
Its year of foundation is unknown. However, since it is identified as a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}} in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448}}, it is presumed to have already existed at least by the {{ill|lt=Engi|Q1374325|WD=Q1374325}} era ({{ill|lt=901|Q23580|WD=Q23580}}-{{ill|lt=923|Q30108|WD=Q30108}})<ref name="散歩">Shizuoka Prefecture Japanese History Education Research Society (ed.), ''A Historical Walk of Shizuoka Prefecture (Historical Walk 22)'', Yamakawa Publishing, 2006, p.67</ref>.

According to the entry for year 5 of {{ill|lt=Emperor Ōjin|Q317997|WD=Q317997}} ({{ill|lt=274|Q33512|WD=Q33512}}) in the ''{{ill|lt=Nihon Shoki|Q813935|WD=Q813935}}'', timber from near this shrine was used by imperial command to build a boat, and because the boat floated up "lightly," the shrine name "Karuno" ("light field") is said to derive from that<ref name="散歩"></ref>.

=== Access ===
* About 3 minutes by car from {{ill|lt=Ōdaira Interchange (Shizuoka)|Q11435260|WD=Q11435260|lt=Ōdaira IC}}.

{{nihongo|'''Karuno Shrine'''|軽野神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Izu Province]].

== Japanese content ==
{{神社
|名称   = Karuno Shrine
|画像={{画像募集中|cat=伊豆市}}
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=11|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|34|56|35.4|N|138|55|54.6|E}}}}

|位置 = {{Coord|34|56|35.4|N|138|55|54.6|E|region:JP-22_scale:10000_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}

{{coord2|34|56|35.4|N|138|55|54.6|E}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}}
}}
'''Karuno Shrine''' (かるのじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in {{ill|lt=Izu, Shizuoka|Q696840|WD=Q696840}}, {{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320|WD=Q131320}}.

=== History ===
Its year of foundation is unknown. However, since it is identified as a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}} in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448}}, it is presumed to have already existed at least by the {{ill|lt=Engi|Q1374325|WD=Q1374325}} era ({{ill|lt=901|Q23580|WD=Q23580}}-{{ill|lt=923|Q30108|WD=Q30108}})<ref name="散歩">Shizuoka Prefecture Japanese History Education Research Society (ed.), ''A Historical Walk of Shizuoka Prefecture (Historical Walk 22)'', Yamakawa Publishing, 2006, p.67</ref>.

According to the entry for year 5 of {{ill|lt=Emperor Ōjin|Q317997|WD=Q317997}} ({{ill|lt=274|Q33512|WD=Q33512}}) in the ''{{ill|lt=Nihon Shoki|Q813935|WD=Q813935}}'', timber from near this shrine was used by imperial command to build a boat, and because the boat floated up "lightly," the shrine name "Karuno" ("light field") is said to derive from that<ref name="散歩"></ref>.

=== Access ===
* About 3 minutes by car from {{ill|lt=Ōdaira Interchange (Shizuoka)|Q11435260|WD=Q11435260|lt=Ōdaira IC}}.

=== Notes ===

{{nihongo|'''Karuno Shrine'''|軽野神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Izu Province]].

'''Karuno Shrine''' (かるのじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Izu, Shizuoka|Q696840|WD=Q696840|1=Izu, Shizuoka}}, {{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320|WD=Q131320|1=Shizuoka Prefecture}}.

=== History ===
Its year of foundation is unknown. However, since it is identified as a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}}, it is presumed to have already existed at least by the {{ill|lt=Engi|Q1374325|WD=Q1374325|1=Engi (era)}} era ({{ill|lt=901|Q23580|WD=Q23580|1=901}}-{{ill|lt=923|Q30108|WD=Q30108|1=923}})<ref name="散歩">Shizuoka Prefecture Japanese History Education Research Society (ed.), ''A Historical Walk of Shizuoka Prefecture (Historical Walk 22)'', Yamakawa Publishing, 2006, p.67</ref>.

According to the entry for year 5 of {{ill|lt=Emperor Ōjin|Q317997|WD=Q317997|1=Emperor Ōjin}} ({{ill|lt=274|Q33512|WD=Q33512|1=274}}) in the ''{{ill|lt=Nihon Shoki|Q813935|WD=Q813935|1=Nihon Shoki}}'', timber from near this shrine was used by imperial command to build a boat, and because the boat floated up "lightly," the shrine name "Karuno" ("light field") is said to derive from that<ref name="散歩"></ref>.

=== Access ===
* About 3 minutes by car from {{ill|lt=Ōdaira Interchange (Shizuoka)|Q11435260|WD=Q11435260|lt=Ōdaira IC|1=Odaira interchange}}.

'''Karuno Shrine''' (かるのじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Izu, Shizuoka|Q696840|WD=Q696840|1=Izu, Shizuoka}}, {{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320|WD=Q131320|1=Shizuoka Prefecture}}.

=== History ===
Its year of foundation is unknown. However, since it is identified as a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}}, it is presumed to have already existed at least by the {{ill|lt=Engi|Q1374325|WD=Q1374325|1=Engi (era)}} era ({{ill|lt=901|Q23580|WD=Q23580|1=901}}-{{ill|lt=923|Q30108|WD=Q30108|1=923}})<ref name="散歩">Shizuoka Prefecture Japanese History Education Research Society (ed.), ''A Historical Walk of Shizuoka Prefecture (Historical Walk 22)'', Yamakawa Publishing, 2006, p.67</ref>.

According to the entry for year 5 of {{ill|lt=Emperor Ōjin|Q317997|WD=Q317997|1=Emperor Ōjin}} ({{ill|lt=274|Q33512|WD=Q33512|1=274}}) in the ''{{ill|lt=Nihon Shoki|Q813935|WD=Q813935|1=Nihon Shoki}}'', timber from near this shrine was used by imperial command to build a boat, and because the boat floated up "lightly," the shrine name "Karuno" ("light field") is said to derive from that<ref name="散歩"></ref>.

=== Access ===
* About 3 minutes by car from {{ill|lt=Ōdaira Interchange (Shizuoka)|Q11435260|WD=Q11435260|lt=Ōdaira IC|1=Odaira interchange}}.

'''Karuno Shrine''' (かるのじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Izu, Shizuoka|Q696840|WD=Q696840|1=Izu, Shizuoka}}, {{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320|WD=Q131320|1=Shizuoka Prefecture}}.

=== History ===
Its year of foundation is unknown. However, since it is identified as a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}}, it is presumed to have already existed at least by the {{ill|lt=Engi|Q1374325|WD=Q1374325|1=Engi (era)}} era ({{ill|lt=901|Q23580|WD=Q23580|1=901}}-{{ill|lt=923|Q30108|WD=Q30108|1=923}})<ref name="散歩">Shizuoka Prefecture Japanese History Education Research Society (ed.), ''A Historical Walk of Shizuoka Prefecture (Historical Walk 22)'', Yamakawa Publishing, 2006, p.67</ref>.

According to the entry for year 5 of {{ill|lt=Emperor Ōjin|Q317997|WD=Q317997|1=Emperor Ōjin}} ({{ill|lt=274|Q33512|WD=Q33512|1=274}}) in the ''{{ill|lt=Nihon Shoki|Q813935|WD=Q813935|1=Nihon Shoki}}'', timber from near this shrine was used by imperial command to build a boat, and because the boat floated up "lightly," the shrine name "Karuno" ("light field") is said to derive from that<ref name="散歩"></ref>.

=== Access ===
* About 3 minutes by car from {{ill|lt=Ōdaira Interchange (Shizuoka)|Q11435260|WD=Q11435260|lt=Ōdaira IC|1=Odaira interchange}}.

'''Karuno Shrine''' (かるのじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Izu, Shizuoka|Q696840|WD=Q696840|1=Izu, Shizuoka}}, {{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320|WD=Q131320|1=Shizuoka Prefecture}}.

=== History ===
Its year of foundation is unknown. However, since it is identified as a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}}, it is presumed to have already existed at least by the {{ill|lt=Engi|Q1374325|WD=Q1374325|1=Engi (era)}} era ({{ill|lt=901|Q23580|WD=Q23580|1=901}}-{{ill|lt=923|Q30108|WD=Q30108|1=923}})<ref name="散歩">Shizuoka Prefecture Japanese History Education Research Society (ed.), ''A Historical Walk of Shizuoka Prefecture (Historical Walk 22)'', Yamakawa Publishing, 2006, p.67</ref>.

According to the entry for year 5 of {{ill|lt=Emperor Ōjin|Q317997|WD=Q317997|1=Emperor Ōjin}} ({{ill|lt=274|Q33512|WD=Q33512|1=274}}) in the ''{{ill|lt=Nihon Shoki|Q813935|WD=Q813935|1=Nihon Shoki}}'', timber from near this shrine was used by imperial command to build a boat, and because the boat floated up "lightly," the shrine name "Karuno" ("light field") is said to derive from that<ref name="散歩"></ref>.

=== Access ===
* About 3 minutes by car from {{ill|lt=Ōdaira Interchange (Shizuoka)|Q11435260|WD=Q11435260|lt=Ōdaira IC|1=Odaira interchange}}.

'''Karuno Shrine''' (かるのじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Izu, Shizuoka|Q696840|WD=Q696840|1=Izu, Shizuoka}}, {{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320|WD=Q131320|1=Shizuoka Prefecture}}.

=== History ===
Its year of foundation is unknown. However, since it is identified as a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}}, it is presumed to have already existed at least by the {{ill|lt=Engi|Q1374325|WD=Q1374325|1=Engi (era)}} era ({{ill|lt=901|Q23580|WD=Q23580|1=901}}-{{ill|lt=923|Q30108|WD=Q30108|1=923}})<ref name="散歩">Shizuoka Prefecture Japanese History Education Research Society (ed.), ''A Historical Walk of Shizuoka Prefecture (Historical Walk 22)'', Yamakawa Publishing, 2006, p.67</ref>.

According to the entry for year 5 of {{ill|lt=Emperor Ōjin|Q317997|WD=Q317997|1=Emperor Ōjin}} ({{ill|lt=274|Q33512|WD=Q33512|1=274}}) in the ''{{ill|lt=Nihon Shoki|Q813935|WD=Q813935|1=Nihon Shoki}}'', timber from near this shrine was used by imperial command to build a boat, and because the boat floated up "lightly," the shrine name "Karuno" ("light field") is said to derive from that<ref name="散歩"></ref>.

=== Access ===
* About 3 minutes by car from {{ill|lt=Ōdaira Interchange (Shizuoka)|Q11435260|WD=Q11435260|lt=Ōdaira IC|1=Odaira interchange}}.

'''Karuno Shrine''' (かるのじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Izu, Shizuoka|Q696840|WD=Q696840|1=Izu, Shizuoka}}, {{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320|WD=Q131320|1=Shizuoka Prefecture}}.

=== History ===
Its year of foundation is unknown. However, since it is identified as a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}}, it is presumed to have already existed at least by the {{ill|lt=Engi|Q1374325|WD=Q1374325|1=Engi (era)}} era ({{ill|lt=901|Q23580|WD=Q23580|1=901}}-{{ill|lt=923|Q30108|WD=Q30108|1=923}})<ref name="散歩">Shizuoka Prefecture Japanese History Education Research Society (ed.), ''A Historical Walk of Shizuoka Prefecture (Historical Walk 22)'', Yamakawa Publishing, 2006, p.67</ref>.

According to the entry for year 5 of {{ill|lt=Emperor Ōjin|Q317997|WD=Q317997|1=Emperor Ōjin}} ({{ill|lt=274|Q33512|WD=Q33512|1=274}}) in the ''{{ill|lt=Nihon Shoki|Q813935|WD=Q813935|1=Nihon Shoki}}'', timber from near this shrine was used by imperial command to build a boat, and because the boat floated up "lightly," the shrine name "Karuno" ("light field") is said to derive from that<ref name="散歩"></ref>.

=== Access ===
* About 3 minutes by car from {{ill|lt=Ōdaira Interchange (Shizuoka)|Q11435260|WD=Q11435260|lt=Ōdaira IC|1=Odaira interchange}}.

'''Karuno Shrine''' (かるのじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Izu, Shizuoka|Q696840|WD=Q696840|1=Izu, Shizuoka}}, {{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320|WD=Q131320|1=Shizuoka Prefecture}}.

=== History ===
Its year of foundation is unknown. However, since it is identified as a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}}, it is presumed to have already existed at least by the {{ill|lt=Engi|Q1374325|WD=Q1374325|1=Engi (era)}} era ({{ill|lt=901|Q23580|WD=Q23580|1=901}}-{{ill|lt=923|Q30108|WD=Q30108|1=923}})<ref name="散歩">Shizuoka Prefecture Japanese History Education Research Society (ed.), ''A Historical Walk of Shizuoka Prefecture (Historical Walk 22)'', Yamakawa Publishing, 2006, p.67</ref>.

According to the entry for year 5 of {{ill|lt=Emperor Ōjin|Q317997|WD=Q317997|1=Emperor Ōjin}} ({{ill|lt=274|Q33512|WD=Q33512|1=274}}) in the ''{{ill|lt=Nihon Shoki|Q813935|WD=Q813935|1=Nihon Shoki}}'', timber from near this shrine was used by imperial command to build a boat, and because the boat floated up "lightly," the shrine name "Karuno" ("light field") is said to derive from that<ref name="散歩"></ref>.

=== Access ===
* About 3 minutes by car from {{ill|lt=Ōdaira Interchange (Shizuoka)|Q11435260|WD=Q11435260|lt=Ōdaira IC|1=Odaira interchange}}.

'''Karuno Shrine''' (かるのじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Izu, Shizuoka|Q696840|WD=Q696840|1=Izu, Shizuoka}}, {{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320|WD=Q131320|1=Shizuoka Prefecture}}.

=== History ===
Its year of foundation is unknown. However, since it is identified as a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}}, it is presumed to have already existed at least by the {{ill|lt=Engi|Q1374325|WD=Q1374325|1=Engi (era)}} era ({{ill|lt=901|Q23580|WD=Q23580|1=901}}-{{ill|lt=923|Q30108|WD=Q30108|1=923}})<ref name="散歩">Shizuoka Prefecture Japanese History Education Research Society (ed.), ''A Historical Walk of Shizuoka Prefecture (Historical Walk 22)'', Yamakawa Publishing, 2006, p.67</ref>.

According to the entry for year 5 of {{ill|lt=Emperor Ōjin|Q317997|WD=Q317997|1=Emperor Ōjin}} ({{ill|lt=274|Q33512|WD=Q33512|1=274}}) in the ''{{ill|lt=Nihon Shoki|Q813935|WD=Q813935|1=Nihon Shoki}}'', timber from near this shrine was used by imperial command to build a boat, and because the boat floated up "lightly," the shrine name "Karuno" ("light field") is said to derive from that<ref name="散歩"></ref>.

=== Access ===
* About 3 minutes by car from {{ill|lt=Ōdaira Interchange (Shizuoka)|Q11435260|WD=Q11435260|lt=Ōdaira IC|1=Odaira interchange}}.

'''Karuno Shrine''' (かるのじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Izu, Shizuoka|Q696840|WD=Q696840|1=Izu, Shizuoka}}, {{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320|WD=Q131320|1=Shizuoka Prefecture}}.

=== History ===
Its year of foundation is unknown. However, since it is identified as a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}}, it is presumed to have already existed at least by the {{ill|lt=Engi|Q1374325|WD=Q1374325|1=Engi (era)}} era ({{ill|lt=901|Q23580|WD=Q23580|1=901}}-{{ill|lt=923|Q30108|WD=Q30108|1=923}})<ref name="散歩">Shizuoka Prefecture Japanese History Education Research Society (ed.), ''A Historical Walk of Shizuoka Prefecture (Historical Walk 22)'', Yamakawa Publishing, 2006, p.67</ref>.

According to the entry for year 5 of {{ill|lt=Emperor Ōjin|Q317997|WD=Q317997|1=Emperor Ōjin}} ({{ill|lt=274|Q33512|WD=Q33512|1=274}}) in the ''{{ill|lt=Nihon Shoki|Q813935|WD=Q813935|1=Nihon Shoki}}'', timber from near this shrine was used by imperial command to build a boat, and because the boat floated up "lightly," the shrine name "Karuno" ("light field") is said to derive from that<ref name="散歩"></ref>.

=== Access ===
* About 3 minutes by car from {{ill|lt=Ōdaira Interchange (Shizuoka)|Q11435260|WD=Q11435260|lt=Ōdaira IC|1=Odaira interchange}}.

{{nihongo|'''Karuno Shrine'''|軽野神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Izu Province]].

'''Karuno Shrine''' (かるのじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Izu, Shizuoka|Q696840|WD=Q696840|1=Izu, Shizuoka}}, {{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320|WD=Q131320|1=Shizuoka Prefecture}}.

=== History ===
Its year of foundation is unknown. However, since it is identified as a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}}, it is presumed to have already existed at least by the {{ill|lt=Engi|Q1374325|WD=Q1374325|1=Engi (era)}} era ({{ill|lt=901|Q23580|WD=Q23580|1=901}}-{{ill|lt=923|Q30108|WD=Q30108|1=923}})<ref name="散歩">Shizuoka Prefecture Japanese History Education Research Society (ed.), ''A Historical Walk of Shizuoka Prefecture (Historical Walk 22)'', Yamakawa Publishing, 2006, p.67</ref>.

According to the entry for year 5 of {{ill|lt=Emperor Ōjin|Q317997|WD=Q317997|1=Emperor Ōjin}} ({{ill|lt=274|Q33512|WD=Q33512|1=274}}) in the ''{{ill|lt=Nihon Shoki|Q813935|WD=Q813935|1=Nihon Shoki}}'', timber from near this shrine was used by imperial command to build a boat, and because the boat floated up "lightly," the shrine name "Karuno" ("light field") is said to derive from that<ref name="散歩"></ref>.

=== Access ===
* About 3 minutes by car from {{ill|lt=Ōdaira Interchange (Shizuoka)|Q11435260|WD=Q11435260|lt=Ōdaira IC|1=Odaira interchange}}.

'''Karuno Shrine''' (かるのじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Izu, Shizuoka|Q696840|WD=Q696840|1=Izu, Shizuoka}}, {{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320|WD=Q131320|1=Shizuoka Prefecture}}.

=== History ===
Its year of foundation is unknown. However, since it is identified as a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}}, it is presumed to have already existed at least by the {{ill|lt=Engi|Q1374325|WD=Q1374325|1=Engi (era)}} era ({{ill|lt=901|Q23580|WD=Q23580|1=901}}-{{ill|lt=923|Q30108|WD=Q30108|1=923}})<ref name="散歩">Shizuoka Prefecture Japanese History Education Research Society (ed.), ''A Historical Walk of Shizuoka Prefecture (Historical Walk 22)'', Yamakawa Publishing, 2006, p.67</ref>.

According to the entry for year 5 of {{ill|lt=Emperor Ōjin|Q317997|WD=Q317997|1=Emperor Ōjin}} ({{ill|lt=274|Q33512|WD=Q33512|1=274}}) in the ''{{ill|lt=Nihon Shoki|Q813935|WD=Q813935|1=Nihon Shoki}}'', timber from near this shrine was used by imperial command to build a boat, and because the boat floated up "lightly," the shrine name "Karuno" ("light field") is said to derive from that<ref name="散歩"></ref>.

=== Access ===
* About 3 minutes by car from {{ill|lt=Ōdaira Interchange (Shizuoka)|Q11435260|WD=Q11435260|lt=Ōdaira IC|1=Odaira interchange}}.

'''Karuno Shrine''' (かるのじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Izu, Shizuoka|Q696840|WD=Q696840|1=Izu, Shizuoka}}, {{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320|WD=Q131320|1=Shizuoka Prefecture}}.

=== History ===
Its year of foundation is unknown. However, since it is identified as a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}}, it is presumed to have already existed at least by the {{ill|lt=Engi|Q1374325|WD=Q1374325|1=Engi (era)}} era ({{ill|lt=901|Q23580|WD=Q23580|1=901}}-{{ill|lt=923|Q30108|WD=Q30108|1=923}})<ref name="散歩">Shizuoka Prefecture Japanese History Education Research Society (ed.), ''A Historical Walk of Shizuoka Prefecture (Historical Walk 22)'', Yamakawa Publishing, 2006, p.67</ref>.

According to the entry for year 5 of {{ill|lt=Emperor Ōjin|Q317997|WD=Q317997|1=Emperor Ōjin}} ({{ill|lt=274|Q33512|WD=Q33512|1=274}}) in the ''{{ill|lt=Nihon Shoki|Q813935|WD=Q813935|1=Nihon Shoki}}'', timber from near this shrine was used by imperial command to build a boat, and because the boat floated up "lightly," the shrine name "Karuno" ("light field") is said to derive from that<ref name="散歩"></ref>.

=== Access ===
* About 3 minutes by car from {{ill|lt=Ōdaira Interchange (Shizuoka)|Q11435260|WD=Q11435260|lt=Ōdaira IC|1=Odaira interchange}}.

'''Karuno Shrine''' (かるのじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|qid=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Izu, Shizuoka|Q696840|qid=Q696840|1=Izu, Shizuoka}}, {{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320|qid=Q131320|1=Shizuoka Prefecture}}.

=== History ===
Its year of foundation is unknown. However, since it is identified as a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286}} in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|qid=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}}, it is presumed to have already existed at least by the {{ill|lt=Engi|Q1374325|qid=Q1374325|1=Engi (era)}} era ({{ill|lt=901|Q23580|qid=Q23580|1=901}}-{{ill|lt=923|Q30108|qid=Q30108|1=923}})<ref name="散歩">Shizuoka Prefecture Japanese History Education Research Society (ed.), ''A Historical Walk of Shizuoka Prefecture (Historical Walk 22)'', Yamakawa Publishing, 2006, p.67</ref>.

According to the entry for year 5 of {{ill|lt=Emperor Ōjin|Q317997|qid=Q317997|1=Emperor Ōjin}} ({{ill|lt=274|Q33512|qid=Q33512|1=274}}) in the ''{{ill|lt=Nihon Shoki|Q813935|qid=Q813935|1=Nihon Shoki}}'', timber from near this shrine was used by imperial command to build a boat, and because the boat floated up "lightly," the shrine name "Karuno" ("light field") is said to derive from that<ref name="散歩"></ref>.

=== Access ===
* About 3 minutes by car from {{ill|lt=Ōdaira Interchange (Shizuoka)|Q11435260|qid=Q11435260|lt=Ōdaira IC|1=Odaira interchange}}.

'''Karuno Shrine''' (かるのじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|qid=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Izu, Shizuoka|Q696840|qid=Q696840|1=Izu, Shizuoka}}, {{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320|qid=Q131320|1=Shizuoka Prefecture}}.

=== History ===
Its year of foundation is unknown. However, since it is identified as a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286}} in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|qid=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}}, it is presumed to have already existed at least by the {{ill|lt=Engi|Q1374325|qid=Q1374325|1=Engi (era)}} era ({{ill|lt=901|Q23580|qid=Q23580|1=901}}-{{ill|lt=923|Q30108|qid=Q30108|1=923}})<ref name="散歩">Shizuoka Prefecture Japanese History Education Research Society (ed.), ''A Historical Walk of Shizuoka Prefecture (Historical Walk 22)'', Yamakawa Publishing, 2006, p.67</ref>.

According to the entry for year 5 of {{ill|lt=Emperor Ōjin|Q317997|qid=Q317997|1=Emperor Ōjin}} ({{ill|lt=274|Q33512|qid=Q33512|1=274}}) in the ''{{ill|lt=Nihon Shoki|Q813935|qid=Q813935|1=Nihon Shoki}}'', timber from near this shrine was used by imperial command to build a boat, and because the boat floated up "lightly," the shrine name "Karuno" ("light field") is said to derive from that<ref name="散歩"></ref>.

=== Access ===
* About 3 minutes by car from {{ill|lt=Ōdaira Interchange (Shizuoka)|Q11435260|qid=Q11435260|lt=Ōdaira IC|1=Odaira interchange}}.
